content.text: Ephemeral Preview Environment Provisioning

An ephemeral preview environment is a fully functional, isolated deployment of the application spun up automatically for every pull request and torn down when the PR is merged or closed. For GenAI services, preview environments let reviewers interact with a prompt change or a new capability end-to-end without deploying to staging — enabling quality review that code review alone cannot provide.

The key design constraint for GenAI preview environments is cost control: running real LLM API calls for every PR would cost hundreds of dollars per month at scale. The solution is a stub LLM sidecar that returns canned responses at near-zero cost, validating everything except actual model output quality, which is covered by the CI eval suite.

attrs.alt: Ephemeral preview environment lifecycle and per-PR resource layout

content.text: GitHub Actions Provisioning Workflow

# .github/workflows/pr-preview.yml
name: PR Preview Environment

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]
  pull_request_target:
    types: [closed]

env:
  PREVIEW_NAMESPACE: pr-${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
  PREVIEW_HOST: pr-${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}.preview.internal

jobs:
  provision:
    if: github.event.action != 'closed'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      pull-requests: write
      id-token: write

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Authenticate to cluster
        uses: azure/k8s-set-context@v3
        with:
          kubeconfig: ${{ secrets.PREVIEW_KUBECONFIG }}

      - name: Build and push preview image
        run: |
          docker build -t registry.internal/genai-service:pr-${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} .
          docker push registry.internal/genai-service:pr-${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}

      - name: Provision namespace and resources
        run: |
          helm upgrade --install preview-${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} \
            charts/genai-preview \
            --namespace ${{ env.PREVIEW_NAMESPACE }} \
            --create-namespace \
            --set image.tag=pr-${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} \
            --set ingress.host=${{ env.PREVIEW_HOST }} \
            --set llm.mode=stub \
            --set ttl.hours=24 \
            --wait --timeout=5m

      - name: Post preview URL to PR
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const url = 'https://${{ env.PREVIEW_HOST }}'
            github.rest.issues.createComment({
              issue_number: context.issue.number,
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              body: [
                '## Preview Environment Ready',
                '',
                `**URL:** ${url}`,
                '**Mode:** Stub LLM (canned responses, no API cost)',
                '**TTL:** 24 hours from last push',
                '',
                'Test checklist:',
                '- [ ] UI renders correctly',
                '- [ ] RAG answer flow completes end-to-end',
                '- [ ] Error states display correctly',
                '- [ ] Auth flow works',
              ].join('\n')
            })

  teardown:
    if: github.event.action == 'closed'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Delete preview namespace
        run: |
          helm uninstall preview-${{ github.event.pull_request.number }} \
            --namespace ${{ env.PREVIEW_NAMESPACE }} || true
          kubectl delete namespace ${{ env.PREVIEW_NAMESPACE }} --ignore-not-found
Helm Chart for Preview Environments

A dedicated Helm chart for preview environments sets resource quotas, configures the LLM stub, and enforces TTL annotation. It is a slimmer version of the production chart — single replica, no HPA, stub backend.

# charts/genai-preview/values.yaml
image:
  repository: registry.internal/genai-service
  tag: latest
  pullPolicy: Always

replicas: 1   # never HA in preview

resources:
  requests:
    cpu: 200m
    memory: 256Mi
  limits:
    cpu: 500m
    memory: 512Mi

llm:
  mode: stub           # 'stub' | 'real' (default: stub for previews)
  stubPort: 8080

ingress:
  enabled: true
  host: ""             # set per-PR by workflow
  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: preview-ca
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/auth-url: "https://oauth2-proxy.preview.internal/oauth2/auth"
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/auth-signin: "https://oauth2-proxy.preview.internal/oauth2/sign_in"

ttl:
  hours: 24
  annotation: "preview.genai.internal/expires-at"

resourceQuota:
  enabled: true
  hard:
    requests.cpu: "2"
    requests.memory: 2Gi
    limits.cpu: "2"
    limits.memory: 2Gi
    pods: "5"
The LLM Stub Sidecar

The stub sidecar implements the OpenAI API interface and returns deterministic canned responses. The application code is unmodified — it calls the same `openai.OpenAI()` client but pointed at `http://llm-stub:8080` instead of `api.openai.com`. This validates every layer except the model's output quality.

The stub validates that the application correctly parses and renders LLM responses, handles errors, and routes correctly — everything except whether the real model produces a good answer. Combine preview environments (UX validation) with the CI eval suite (output quality validation) for full coverage.
TTL Controller for Abandoned Environments
PRs get abandoned, reverted, or forgotten. Without a TTL controller, preview namespaces accumulate and cost grows. A CronJob checks namespace annotations every 6 hours and deletes expired previews.

# scripts/preview_ttl_controller.py
# Kubernetes CronJob: delete preview namespaces past their TTL.
# Runs every 6 hours in the preview cluster.

import subprocess, json, datetime, logging

log = logging.getLogger(__name__)
TTL_ANNOTATION = "preview.genai.internal/expires-at"

def list_preview_namespaces() -> list[dict]:
    result = subprocess.run(
        ["kubectl", "get", "namespaces", "-o", "json",
         "-l", "app.kubernetes.io/managed-by=genai-preview"],
        capture_output=True, text=True, check=True
    )
    return json.loads(result.stdout)["items"]

def delete_namespace(name: str) -> None:
    subprocess.run(
        ["kubectl", "delete", "namespace", name, "--ignore-not-found"],
        check=True
    )
    log.info("deleted_namespace", namespace=name)

def run_ttl_sweep() -> None:
    now = datetime.datetime.utcnow()
    namespaces = list_preview_namespaces()
    deleted = 0

    for ns in namespaces:
        name = ns["metadata"]["name"]
        annotations = ns["metadata"].get("annotations", {})
        expires_at_str = annotations.get(TTL_ANNOTATION)

        if not expires_at_str:
            log.warning("no_ttl_annotation", namespace=name)
            continue

        expires_at = datetime.datetime.fromisoformat(expires_at_str)
        if now > expires_at:
            log.info("ttl_expired", namespace=name, expired_at=expires_at_str)
            delete_namespace(name)
            deleted += 1

    log.info("ttl_sweep_complete", checked=len(namespaces), deleted=deleted)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)
    run_ttl_sweep()
Summary
Ephemeral preview environments spin up a full application stack per PR and tear down on merge or close — enabling end-to-end UX review without deploying to staging.
Use a stub LLM sidecar (OpenAI-compatible) in preview environments so reviewers can test all application flows at near-zero API cost; real model quality is validated separately by the CI eval suite.
Implement per-namespace ResourceQuotas to cap preview environment resource consumption across concurrent PRs.
Gate preview access with OAuth2 proxy + GitHub SSO so preview URLs are not publicly accessible.
Annotate namespaces with an expiry timestamp and run a CronJob TTL controller every 6 hours to delete abandoned preview environments — never rely solely on PR close events.
Post the preview URL as a PR comment immediately after provisioning, with a structured review checklist, so reviewers know exactly what to validate.
